steps.title: Set up the 2010 baseline
steps.content:
In 2010, let $a$ = advanced skaters and $b$ = intermediate skaters. Since these are the only two categories:
$$a + b = 35$$

steps.title: Apply the growth rates to 2020 values
steps.content:
A percentage increase means multiply by (1 + the rate). A 53% increase means multiply by 1.53; a 44% increase by 1.44; a 49% increase by 1.49. Applying these to each category:
  • Advanced skaters in 2020: $1.53a$
  • Intermediate skaters in 2020: $1.44b$
  • Total skaters in 2020: \$35(1.49)$

steps.title: Build the equation from the constraint
steps.content:
In 2020, all skaters are either advanced or intermediate, so these two groups sum to the total:
$$1.53a + 1.44b = 35(1.49)$$

This matches choice C — C is correct.
